subject: [PATCH bpf v4 2/3] bpf: Avoid faultable build ID reads under mm locks

reviews.author: Ihor Solodrai
reviews.summary: Reviewer Ihor Solodrai questioned the use of trylock() in this patch, suggesting that it would be better to reuse a mechanism already introduced in stack_map_lock_vma(), and asked if factoring out this common mechanism from stackmap.c is worth considering.
reviews.raw_body:
v1 used mmap_read_lock() as the fallback, but Puranjay pointed out [1]
that stackmap can be called when the caller already holds
mmap_lock. So I changed to trylock since v2, similar to non-sleepable
path.

AFAIU this means that the common mechanism needs to support trylock
behavior as in stack_map_lock_vma() in this patch.

Do you think this is worth factoring out of stackmap.c?

[1] https://lore.kernel.org/bpf/m25x611s17.fsf@kernel.org/

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary:
Author asked for clarification on why a trylock is used instead of reusing the mechanism introduced in [2], specifically seeking to understand if the code path can be taken from bpf programs and if they can be attached at functions/code-paths already holding the mmap_lock.

Author is addressing a suggestion by Ihor Solodrai to reuse the mechanism introduced in [2] instead of using trylock(). The author is asking for clarification on whether similar handling will be needed at other places, particularly in the bpf world, before deciding to factor out the mechanism now or wait until later.
reviews.raw_body:
Can you exapnd on the scenario where caller already holds mmap_lock? Is this
code path can be taken from bpf programs and bpf programs can be attached at
functions/code-paths already holding the mmap_lock?

---

Do you think we will need similar handling at more places in future
(particularly in bpf world) then it makes sense to factoring out now? Otherwise
we can wait until we are sure.

reviews.author: Ihor Solodrai
reviews.summary:
Reviewer noted that taking the mmap_lock under a BPF program's execution path may not be safe, citing examples of tracing and iterators over VMAs, and requested reusing the mechanism introduced in another patch to handle this safely

Reviewer questioned the use of trylock() and suggested reusing the mechanism introduced in [2] to avoid premature generalization
reviews.raw_body:
Exactly. For example, tracing BPF programs may attach to mm internals, and
there are also BPF iterators over VMAs (i.e. SEC("iter/task_vma")). And
probably other use-cases.

Bottom line is we can't assume it's safe to take mmap_lock in a code path
reachable from a BPF program.

---

Honestly, I don't know. IMO we shouldn't prematurely generalize a piece of
code with a single user. But I just may be unaware of the others.

reviews.author: Shakeel Butt (author)
reviews.summary: Author acknowledged reviewer's suggestion to reuse a mechanism introduced in [2] instead of using trylock, but decided to leave it as is for now and consider refactoring later.
sentiment_signals: author chose not to implement suggested change
reviews.raw_body:
So, we have two somewhat similar users with trylock being the difference. I
think we can continue as is for now and let's punt the decision to refactoring
and common code when next use-case arise.

2026-05-14.patch_summary: This patch introduces a trylock mechanism to avoid faultable build ID reads under mm locks, instead of reusing the existing mechanism introduced in another commit.
